pr.title: feat: set up BANTAY-TB tuberculosis surveillance platform
pr.body:
## Summary

Sets up the complete BANTAY-TB GIS-based tuberculosis surveillance platform in TECHO_123 with a working Supabase backend.

**What's included:**
- Full React + TypeScript + Vite frontend with Tailwind CSS styling
- 10 Supabase migration files creating all tables, RLS policies, RBAC functions, and triggers
- GIS hotspot detection using DBSCAN spatial clustering algorithm
- DOTS Center locator with interactive Leaflet maps (12 seeded centers)
- Multilingual health education content (English, Filipino, Bisaya — 105 articles)
- Role-based access control: system_admin, tb_coordinator, barangay_admin, health_worker, patient
- Adherence monitoring, SMS pipeline, and AI chatbot edge functions
- Database seeded with 182 barangays, 12 DOTS centers, 105 health articles, 1,351 synthetic TB cases

**Fixes applied:**
- Split `system_admin` enum migration into two files for PostgreSQL compatibility (enum values must be committed before use in functions)
- Extended `disease` enum to include `influenza`, `bronchitis`, `copd` for health education content
- Updated `supabase/config.toml` to match remote DB version (17)
- Removed unused `eslint-disable` directive in i18n test
- Updated `database.types.ts` to include all disease types

**All checks pass:** TypeScript 0 errors, ESLint 0 errors, Vite build success, 4/4 tests passing.

## Review & Testing Checklist for Human

- [ ] Copy `.env.example` to `.env` and fill in your Supabase credentials (URL + anon key + staff invite code)
- [ ] Run `npm install && npm run dev` and verify the landing page loads at localhost:5173
- [ ] Visit `/dots-locator` and confirm 12 DOTS centers appear on the map with distances
- [ ] Visit `/learn` and browse health articles in all 3 languages
- [ ] Visit `/login` and verify the sign-in form renders correctly
- [ ] Try creating a staff account at `/register` with the invite code to test auth flow

### Notes

- The `.env` file is gitignored — credentials are not committed
- The Supabase Edge Functions (chatbot, detect-hotspots, send-adherence-sms) need to be deployed separately via `supabase functions deploy`
- The chatbot edge function requires an `OPENAI_API_KEY` secret in Supabase
- Database migrations have already been applied to the linked Supabase project (bxvxtaseugsbhzwgceqi)

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/56092946289b42f68254638aa3efa407
Requested by: @NotJayDee119
pr.author: devin-ai-integration[bot]
